_comment: Committed connection config — never real secrets; the token is the ${HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN:-} env placeholder (tracked file). The connection authenticates when the variable is set: shell env on devices, the environment's env vars in claude.ai cloud sessions. The :- default keeps the config parseable when unset — the connection then just shows as unavailable in /mcp until the token is provided. The package version is pinned (bump deliberately; the config is auto-approved, so an unpinned latest would be a supply-chain risk). Default binary is the full 127-tool hostinger-api-mcp; set HOSTINGER_MCP_BINARY to a category binary (hostinger-vps-mcp, hostinger-dns-mcp, ...) to keep the tool surface lean per SKILL.md. Upstream states Node v24+; verified working on Node 22. Multi-account setups keep using `claude mcp add -s user` per references/installation.md.
